tweets.text: This week, we joined the world in saying goodbye to Madam Secretary @Madeleine Albright and celebrating her legacy as our first female Secretary of State. Hear from diplomats who worked alongside her as they remember her life and lasting impact: https://t.co/4D2TBXE7T2

.@WhiteHouse Special Assistant to the President Tim Wu highlights the launch of the Declaration for the #FutureOfTheInternet, where the U.S. will work together with 60+ global partners to advance a positive vision for the Internet and digital technologies. https://t.co/KqpRiIHhXM https://t.co/WnJMBfhS3a

We have been uniting people around the world through music diplomacy exchange programs for over 80 years. On International #JazzDay2022, learn more about our legacy of jazz diplomacy programs as well as our latest initiatives in @ECAatState’s DipNote blog: https://t.co/N4lXIPe3Ck https://t.co/GxlL03HPZb

This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to donate more than 300,000 COVID-19 vaccine doses to ​The Gambia, Lesotho, and Sierra Leone. Visit our interactive COVID-19 page to find out where else we've donated vaccines: https://t.co/aftYKkPmoe https://t.co/gSzab5gk03

During today’s briefing @StateDeputySpox condemned today's attacks in Afghanistan, discussed the return of U.S. diplomats to Ukraine, and highlighted today's anniversary of the Chemical Weapons Convention. https://t.co/pkejDZq7Yg
Trees keep the planet healthy, make life richer for all, and create jobs. On #ArborDay, learn how one organization plans to make our planet 5 million trees greener. https://t.co/A9VKUvbL3W

This week, the United States donated an additional 3 million Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to Bangladesh 🇧🇩, with total doses delivered reaching over 64 million. We are working with Bangladesh to vaccinate more people and help the people of Bangladesh live healthier, safer lives.
Sign up to receive "From the Desk of Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken," email updates from the Secretary on key foreign affairs issues. https://t.co/Rgm7xzIXJL https://t.co/GqRrITHDGc

.@POTUS addressing food shortages caused by Russian aggression: Putin’s war, not sanctions, are impacting the harvest of food and disrupting the movement of that food by land and sea to nations around the globe that need it. https://t.co/iIfl4HDOh2

.@POTUS on the Kremlin’s aggression: We’ve seen them leave behind horrifying evidence of their atrocities and war crimes in the areas they tried to control. And as long as the assaults and atrocities continue, we’re going to continue to supply military assistance. https://t.co/EW8S5PPrW6

.@POTUS: I’m sending Congress a supplemental budget request that is going to keep weapons and ammunition flowing without interruption to the brave Ukrainian fighters and continue delivering economic and humanitarian assistance to the Ukrainian people. https://t.co/e2RwPdCyKg https://t.co/wMs71p7PeL

The U.S. government proudly supports LGBTQI+ persons at home & abroad. Highlights:
✅Supporting LGBTQI+ issues at the @UN_HRC
✅Creating an X gender marker option for U.S. passports
✅Raising the progress flag at State for the first time
We have more plans for this #YearOfAction. https://t.co/cbJ1voXMjg

.@SecBlinken testifying before the House Committee on Foreign Affairs: "We’re also making headway on ensuring our diplomats reflect America’s remarkable diversity. This is a tremendous source of strength for our diplomacy." https://t.co/9kJw94hiMe

.@SecBlinken testifying this morning before House of Representatives Subcommittee: This brutal war of aggression being committed by Russia against Ukraine has brought into sharp focus the power and purpose of American diplomacy. https://t.co/JN4NsiDGKh

Live now! @SecBlinken testifies before the House Committee on Foreign Affairs, on “The Department of State and Related Programs FY23 Budget Request.” Also streaming at https://t.co/qbffwmQOJA. https://t.co/HlWgvLatcH

This #GirlsInICT Day we recognize all women and girls who are utilizing their talents and skills to help shape the future of Information and Communication Technology. The girls of today are the technology leaders of tomorrow. https://t.co/mCWhnQ6Pwf
Live now! You can also tune for this briefing on the Declaration for the #FutureOfTheInternet at: https://t.co/61syqNeeHr.

Live now! @SecBlinken testifies before the House Committee on Appropriations, Subcommittee on State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs on “The Department of State and Related Programs FY23 Budget Request.” Also streaming at https://t.co/UiRX749wzt.  https://t.co/wx8HjfdiWb

To modernize American diplomacy, win the competition for talent, and ensure that applicants can present a full picture of their individual qualifications, the State Department is changing the Foreign Service assessment process beginning in June 2022. More: https://t.co/xStpkClsVq

.@POTUS: Freedom endures against all odds in the face of every aggressor because there are always those who will fight for that freedom. In the 20th and 21st century, freedom had no greater champion than @Madeleine Korbel Albright. Full eulogy remarks: https://t.co/QwBAj11eNc https://t.co/4TmuzdyCnl

On Holocaust Remembrance Day, #YomHashoah, we pause to remember the lives mercilessly taken and the suffering of survivors. We are committed to defending human rights by countering antisemitism and other forms of hate. #NeverForget https://t.co/Aj8VuYUCj2

Since August 2021, the United States has helped facilitate #COVAX’s provision of over 1 million safe and effective COVID-19 vaccine doses to the West Bank and Gaza. We are committed to saving lives in the battle against COVID-19.

In June, @POTUS, @SecBlinken, and leaders from throughout the Americas will come together for the 9th @SummitAmericas. Join conversations and connect with others in building a sustainable, resilient, and equitable hemisphere in a new LinkedIn community. https://t.co/DNZ35uzHz6

Former Secretary @HillaryClinton: When dictators dragged their feet, or ambassadors filibustered, @Madeleine never hesitated to speak up…A dozen times a day she would ask her team, what's next? Turning her boundless energy and intellect to yet another crucial global challenge. https://t.co/N1IeNP8BoD

.@madeleine Albright was known for so many things, and one of them was the pins she wore, often to convey messages about foreign policy. Check out @NMADmuseum’s exhibit, Read My Pins: The Madeleine Albright Collection, which features more than 200 of them. https://t.co/ecffM3O5uT
.@SecBlinken: We have diplomats going back to Ukraine this week as we speak to begin the process of looking to re-open the embassy in Kyiv. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/t5Hglnx8CG

.@SecBlinken honors former Secretary of State @Madeleine Albright: She was a friend to me, a mentor to me, someone I sought counsel in. Few diplomats have so clearly embodied the ideals for which our country stands or done more to project them around the world. https://t.co/7iNFpGkfYw
.@SecBlinken on the release of former U.S. Marine Trevor Reed from Russia: We are deeply grateful to our allies and partners who helped in this effort to bring him home. https://t.co/vOClee9aty

Live now! @SecBlinken testifies before the Senate Committee on Appropriations, Subcommittee on State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs on “The Department of State and Related Programs FY23 Budget Request”. Also streaming at https://t.co/tr2TWnun9h. https://t.co/Iv7MNtDmxb

Today, we honor the life of former Secretary of State Madeleine Korbel Albright. The Washington National Cathedral will host a funeral service beginning at approximately 11 a.m. EDT. Watch live here: https://t.co/yv6N27O12i.

As we say goodbye to @Madeleine Albright today, we remember her legacy as the 64th U.S. Secretary of State — the first woman to hold this position, who mentored a generation of diplomats. Her memory will live on in the work we do around the world. https://t.co/cLIjuwAucM

Live now!  @USAID and @PowerUSAID launch three flagship projects to help close the $40 trillion global infrastructure gap as a part of @POTUS's global infrastructure initiative. https://t.co/kAJ1GK1R9m

Today at 9 a.m. EDT, @USAID and @PowerUSAID will launch three flagship projects to help close the $40 trillion global infrastructure gap as a part of @POTUS's global infrastructure initiative. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/uvlG4EWlG8

.@SecBlinken discusses an agenda set in place to meet demands in diplomacy: “We’re making headway on ensuring that our diplomats reflect America’s remarkable diversity, which is one of our greatest strengths, including in our diplomacy.” https://t.co/ZptrHjyvjM
.@SecBlinken: Moscow’s war of aggression against Ukraine has underscored the power and purpose of American diplomacy. https://t.co/H51QOM7yRb

From the first vaccine against smallpox to the newest vaccines to prevent severe cases of COVID-19, vaccines save lives. During #WorldImmunizationWeek, the U.S. is proud to celebrate the 529 million COVID-19 vaccine doses we have donated to over 110 countries and economies. https://t.co/X5w3AmgsUZ

.@SecBlinken: This is not about advancing the goals of any one administration, any one party. It’s about refocusing our mission and purpose on the forces that really affect the lives of our fellow citizens – their livelihoods, their security – for decades to come. https://t.co/nYWWXA1x7K

.@StateDeptSpox outlines improvements to the Foreign Service selection process: The Department is moving away from the Foreign Service Officer Test as a pass/fail gateway test and expanding focus on each candidate’s education and experience for a more holistic evaluation. https://t.co/DV7hw3icaL

.@StateDeptSpox: The United States is deeply concerned by the Tunisian president’s decision to unilaterally restructure Tunisia’s Independent High Authority for Elections. We remain committed to supporting the Tunisian people in their democratic path. https://t.co/ebKg7FR6T0
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/3sSt1oIJzi. https://t.co/kbfHjoqgBm

.@EconatState's Tarek Fahmy to young entrepreneurs: "#WorldIPDay is an opportunity to find out how intellectual property rights can transform creative ideas into reality, generate income, create new jobs, and make a positive impact around the world.” https://t.co/tYb9ahYqvk

Live now! @SecBlinken testifies before the Senate Committee on Foreign Relations on “The Department of State and Related Programs FY23 Budget Request,” in Washington, D.C. Also streaming on YouTube at https://t.co/lzypMYZt8N. https://t.co/wiz0Rk1f4o

On World Intellectual Property Day, the U.S. celebrates the innovators and creators who enrich our lives. This year’s #WorldIPDay highlights emerging leaders and their contributions to making IP rights stronger, more competitive, and resilient. https://t.co/hZ5PfcCtJT

.@SecBlinken: When it comes to Russia’s war aims, Russia is failing, Ukraine is succeeding. Russia has sought as its principal aim to totally subjugate Ukraine — to take away its sovereignty, to take away its independence. That has failed. https://t.co/CMjLFgPokc https://t.co/XMlO40BoZe

This past weekend @glifaa celebrated their 30th anniversary at their annual Pink Party. In honor of their work to advance LGBTQI+ pride in the foreign affairs community, @MayorBowser and her team surprised them by proclaiming Saturday “glifaa Day” in the District of Columbia. https://t.co/U23gTeD9ji

It’s #WorldImmunizationWeek, and it’s never been more important to emphasize this simple truth: Vaccines are a safe and effective way to protect against deadly diseases, look out for others, and help us live longer, healthier lives. #LongLifeForAll More: https://t.co/Z7s1BYgV1m https://t.co/G4Q8V3ovNU

.@SecBlinken on his meeting in Kyiv with Ukrainian President @ZelenskyyUa: We expressed deep admiration for his leadership, for the extraordinary courage of Ukrainians in standing up to and pushing back this Russian aggression. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/ao6jbQlMi5

.@SecBlinken on his visit to Kyiv: “We had an opportunity to demonstrate directly our strong support, our strong ongoing support for the Ukrainian Government and for the Ukrainian people.” More: https://t.co/CMjLFgPokc #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/0MjnqZXq3C

Today on #WorldMalariaDay, we look at @USAID’s support for a groundbreaking new vaccine that promises to be a game changer in sub-Saharan Africa. We remain committed to working with our partners to fight this disease. Malaria is preventable and curable. https://t.co/5ABoNYI22M

The Russian government and Vladimir Putin work hard to conceal and deflect involvement in chemical attacks on civilians worldwide. Read how they did that and more in the case of British citizen Sergei Skripal: https://t.co/Yxm0BtTiOt. https://t.co/7SYJOnQgJJ

This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to donate nearly 100,00 COVID-19 vaccine doses to the people of Senegal. To see where else we've delivered COVID-19 vaccines in Sub-Saharan Africa and around the world, visit: https://t.co/hp7Zy7y5AZ. https://t.co/4YliE3r2oH

The #HumanRightsReport is used by journalists, lawyers, academics, and activists to promote respect for human rights. Providing the #HRR as a vital public resource reflects our commitment to putting human rights at the center of U.S. foreign policy. https://t.co/Uzf7FUXJ92 https://t.co/J35Ec7ihbU

.@ClimateEnvoy John Kerry at #OurOcean2022: "We can win that fight, but it requires us to change and change now. It requires us to rock the boat, and refuse to believe we are the captives of the status quo. It’s the only way to win the battle." More: https://t.co/mMvLc5ObXz https://t.co/igaimBsniV
On this Orthodox Easter, we wish Orthodox Christians and all who celebrate a joyful day. May your Easter celebrations and observances bring hope for a peaceful year. https://t.co/93EUvqSvNq

.@POTUS: Our unity at home, our unity with our Allies and partners, and our unity with the Ukrainian people is sending an unmistakable message to Putin: He will never succeed in dominating and occupying all of Ukraine. https://t.co/V4ltUz5EAW

.@POTUS announces that the U.S. will ban Russian-affiliated ships from our ports, as they did in Europe. "That means no ship that sails under the Russian flag or that is owned or operated by Russian interests will be allowed to dock in a U.S. port or access our shores. None." https://t.co/psby0ysynp

This past year, U.S. leadership has catalyzed significant successes to protect the ocean, restore biodiversity, end plastic pollution, and provide vaccines & lifesaving treatments to beat COVID-19. Read more from @SciDiplomacyUSA Assistant Secretary Medina.https://t.co/4J4o3QKByq

.@POTUS: We’re going to continue to stand with the brave and proud people of Ukraine. We will never fail in our determination to defend freedom and oppose tyranny. It’s as simple as that. https://t.co/rJsWcex2LH

.@POTUS announces a new program, “Unite for Ukraine,” to enable Ukrainians seeking refuge to come directly from Europe to the United States. "This program will be fast. It will be streamlined." https://t.co/IJl844lCIE

This week, the United States partnered with #COVAX to donate an additional 1.7 million doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to Vietnam, bringing our total donation to more than 39 million doses. The United States supports Vietnam’s efforts to end the COVID-19 pandemic. https://t.co/8BaaQw5mjw

.@State_E will travel to Brazil to support our economic agenda through a High-Level Dialogue co-chaired with @UnderSecStateP. He will also engage on energy and health priorities and promote opportunities for diversity and women’s economic empowerment. https://t.co/28muOfZ8LN

.@ClimateEnvoy John Kerry at #OurOcean2022: "We can win that fight, but it requires us to change and change now. It requires us to rock the boat, and refuse to believe we are the captives of the status quo. It’s the only way to win the battle." More: https://t.co/lvPDdfIbO7. https://t.co/E3Kczr1gcH

In today’s briefing, @StateDeputySpox addresses relations on the Korean Peninsula, condemns ongoing violence in Ethiopia and Jerusalem, and reiterates U.S. support for Moldova’s sovereignty and territorial integrity. Read the full transcript here: https://t.co/ByuBU8ObOt

.@ClimateEnvoy John Kerry: The science tells us that every tenth of a degree of warming matters enormously. And every day, that danger is compounded by indifference and inaction. https://t.co/5YMqd4NTh3

.@ClimateEnvoy John Kerry: We still do have time to avoid the worst consequences of the climate crisis. We can create millions of jobs and trillion-dollar new industries. And we can still reach a cleaner, safer, less polluted planet for all of us. https://t.co/wOZdRKNfLO

#EarthDay is an opportunity to raise awareness of the many environmental challenges we face, says @SciDiplomacyUSA Assistant Secretary Medina. Read her Earth Day DipNote blog to learn how we can take action to foster a healthier planet. https://t.co/4J4o3QKByq

The U.S. is doing its part to build a zero-carbon future that creates good jobs and ensures a healthy, livable planet for generations to come. Meet two people on the front lines of the green jobs revolution — Tim from Pittsburgh and Sandra from Bogotá. https://t.co/B4hhM1dXBA

On the 52nd anniversary of #EarthDay, the United States is dedicated to working towards environmental and diplomatic solutions that foster a healthier planet for all. #InvestInOurPlanet More: https://t.co/viIhow0jEB. https://t.co/ThxU9zgR0B

Everyone has a role in protecting Our Planet. We must unite all levels of society. Utilize clean energy. Protect our health and livelihoods. Support sustainable agriculture and fisheries. And restore the planet for future generations. #EarthDay https://t.co/LPFdcamqj9
Live now! Watch a joint press conference with @DeputySecState Wendy Sherman and @eu_eeas Secretary-General Stefano Sannino. https://t.co/WlaVvdVdVy

.@State_E will travel to Santiago, Chile, April 21-23 to engage with government officials, business leaders, and civil society, in support of regional trade and investment objectives, climate and environment programs, and pandemic response and preparedness.https://t.co/n0tGtk2zSc
The Kremlin lies when it has something to hide. Learn more about the State Department's efforts to expose Russia’s disinformation about atrocities in Bucha: https://t.co/fkjSlfbE0F

This week, @SecBlinken visited the #PanamaCanal, which is a great time for a #ThrowbackThursday edition of our DipNote about the history of the Panama Canal and U.S.-Panama Relations. https://t.co/30qh4ZWBMn
On February 24, Russian President Putin launched a “special military operation” in Ukraine. This is the truth about what happened next. https://t.co/XV0j8mIHzT
.@SecBlinken on managing migration in the Western Hemisphere: “Because it affects all of us, this is a challenge that we have to solve together.” https://t.co/f9ZnzEdl0o

On February 24, Russian President Putin launched a “special military operation” in Ukraine. This is the truth about what happened next. https://t.co/XV0j8mr6Il

The #OurOcean2022 Conference has concluded in Palau with 410 commitments worth $16.35 billion. Since the first Our Ocean Conference in 2014, these efforts have mobilized more than 1,800 commitments worth nearly $108 billion. More: https://t.co/FfJ0zGIfxz. https://t.co/tlIaNJT1O5

Putin’s Poison Playbook is designed to deny, distract, and deflect its involvement and responsibility in heinous chemical weapons attacks. More from @ShareAmerica: https://t.co/WL1OA2J8uU. https://t.co/TsEwtrPSsK

Today, @POTUS announced another $800 million to further augment Ukraine’s ability to fight in the east in the Donbas region, including heavy artillery weapons, howitzers, and tactical drones. https://t.co/TuSPmP5NTx
Live Now! @POTUS provides an update on Russia and Ukraine. Also watch on YouTube at https://t.co/bHUD8KViUc.  https://t.co/oddMJyBxwI

.@SecBlinken met with Anticorruption Champion Awardees Carlos Paparoni 🇻🇪, Diana Salazar 🇪🇨, and Juan Francisco Sandoval Alfaro 🇬🇹. The U.S. values these and other partners who push for accountability and combat corruption despite often facing personal and professional risks. https://t.co/QpRce2n2bH

The Russian government and Vladimir Putin work hard to conceal and deflect involvement in chemical attacks on civilians worldwide. Read how they did that and more in the case of British citizen Sergei Skripal: https://t.co/Yxm0BtTiOt. https://t.co/Aid8VEXGEs

.@SecBlinken addressing migration: “We have a regional challenge, a hemispheric challenge, but it’s also a global challenge. By definition, this is a challenge that we have to stand together to meet, work together to meet.” Read his remarks from Panama: https://t.co/3HtWLVOapM

Join @UnderSecPD and @RepAuchincloss for a discussion at the @AtlanticCouncil on how the United States can continue to effectively combat COVID-19 and lead on global public health. Today at 2 p.m. EDT: https://t.co/RbBWcRKppn
Live now! @SecBlinken participates in a joint press availability with @SecMayorkas, Panamanian Foreign Minister @ErikaMouynes, and @MinSegPma Juan Manuel Pino Forero.  https://t.co/8CbfwGmUvB

The United States is the largest humanitarian donor to international organizations in Panama, whose work complements the Government of Panama’s efforts to provide humanitarian assistance and protection to vulnerable migrants and refugees. https://t.co/BfNH5mYIHg

The U.S. will join with 20 Western Hemisphere countries at the April 19-20 Ministerial on Migration and Protection, working toward a collaborative and humane approach to managing migration and protection through the region. More on the Summit: https://t.co/4fa1wyr7HH. https://t.co/xxzhQBXnvy

The United States is Panama's top economic partner and we are the largest direct investor in Panama. Exports of goods and services from Panama to the United States contribute to having more and better jobs in the United States. https://t.co/tDTbT0QLfR
Panama remains a highly valued partner to the United States. Our governments work side-by-side to advance common goals for our peoples and for the region. https://t.co/xbYfnhQ0xZ

.@SecBlinken on COVID-19: No one’s safe till everyone’s safe. And as long as the virus is percolating somewhere, it could be mutating, and we might end up with a variant that defeats the very strong tools that we’ve now put in place to deal with it. https://t.co/5167iRvB4L

Putin's spokesperson Dmitri Peskov has played a key role in propaganda and disinformation campaigns to cover up the Kremlin’s most horrific abuses including the use of chemical weapons and military strikes killing civilians. Learn more here: https://t.co/8k3cLgLAk0

Diversity and representation are key to achieving our U.S. foreign policy objectives. Celebrate #DeafHistoryMonth with us by attending the inaugural Sign@State Symposium on April 21 at 1 p.m. EDT, with opening remarks by @SecBlinken. To register, go to https://t.co/xcB8EnrF5Z. https://t.co/J3YbzZ3ggM

In the latest DipNote, @SciDiplomacyUSA Assistant Secretary Medina summarizes the progress and commitments made to conserve, protect, and strengthen the ocean’s health following a successful #OurOcean2022 conference, co-hosted with the Republic of Palau. https://t.co/bFXP6a18fP

.@StateDeptSpox discusses the U.S.- @ASEAN summit May 12-13: “Our shared interests for the region will continue to underpin our common commitment to advance an Indo-Pacific that is free and open, connected, prosperous, secure, and resilient.” https://t.co/xR2uMNO3p0

Putin’s Poison Playbook is designed to deny, distract, and deflect its involvement and responsibility in heinous chemical weapons attacks. More from @ShareAmerica: https://t.co/WL1OA2rx6k. https://t.co/lbYHjoYpDE

On #WorldHeritageDay, we are announcing a new partnership between @HeritageAtState and @GoogleArts to expand access to cultural heritage sites around the world. Learn more about the platform & @ECAatState's preservation efforts in the latest DipNote blog: https://t.co/AB2fj3RxCk

This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to donate more than 2.1 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to Comoros, Eswatini, Mauritania, and Nigeria. To see where else we've donated COVID-19 vaccines around the world, visit: https://t.co/C0bhVhl2th https://t.co/3QGDD8MZ8R
Many humanitarian organizations are providing urgent assistance to those affected by the crisis in Ukraine. Here are some ways you can help from wherever you are: https://t.co/1ANlO5M6f8
Subscribe to The Week at State, a weekly email newsletter of all the news you missed from the Department of State. https://t.co/gNUp8OngPT https://t.co/6DnU75W7zO
Happy Easter! We wish all who celebrate a blessed and joyful day with family and friends. May this day be filled with peace, hope, and joy. https://t.co/BPbwynfMTw

8 million tons of plastic enter the ocean every year. By 2050, there could be more plastic than fish (by weight) in the ocean. Here are 10 simple things you can do at home, around town, on the water, or anywhere to protect our ocean. @NOAA https://t.co/ifEceg1nK6

It's #NationalParkWeek! Did you know that the State Department partners with @NatlParkService to strengthen the @FulbrightPrgrm in an effort to increase environmental and cultural awareness throughout the world? Learn more and #FindYourPark. https://t.co/pnpewABoOH https://t.co/gMPKfnwKrV
Wishing @SecBlinken a very happy birthday today! Enjoy your day Mr. Secretary. https://t.co/m7h5SWo3Cp

Our ocean needs our help. The United States is committed to protecting this vital resource. Together, we can clean up our ocean, create protected areas where communities and ecosystems can thrive, and ensure a healthy ocean for future generations. Join us. https://t.co/IptDfS5Mo7

This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to donate more than 2.1 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to Comoros, Eswatini, Mauritania, and Nigeria. To see where else we've donated COVID-19 vaccines around the world, visit: https://t.co/C0bhVh3r4H. https://t.co/vtr5APKRH1

This week the U.S. co-hosted the #OurOcean2022 conference with the Republic of Palau. Partners from around the world came together to increase the ocean’s resilience to the climate crisis and safeguard its health for generations to come. https://t.co/p8X0CwNAa8 https://t.co/H38vByCE6s

This week, the Department released our first-ever Equity Action Plan—a roadmap to advance racial equity and support for underserved communities worldwide. The U.S. is committed to rooting its foreign policy in equity and inclusion. https://t.co/L6xBFQmtKJ https://t.co/KWe2vDUMIC

This year marks the 50th anniversary of Title IX, the United States’ landmark legislation that afforded equal opportunity for women in sport and education. To learn more, join @ECAatState's panel discussion on Gender Equity in Sport. https://t.co/W48P9uqiRG https://t.co/y7Es4SV3o0

The #HumanRightsReport is used by journalists, lawyers, academics, and activists to promote respect for human rights. Providing the #HRR as a vital public resource reflects our commitment to putting human rights at the center of U.S. foreign policy. https://t.co/Uzf7FUXJ92 https://t.co/yJZcGA4zzB

.@SecBlinken: “Governments that violate human rights are almost always the same ones that flout other key parts of that order – such as invading, coercing, and threatening other countries, or breaking trade rules.” The U.S. will continue take a stand for human rights for all. https://t.co/NERN0snR3P

.@SecBlinken discusses some of the most alarming findings of the #HumanRightReports that covers events of 2021, such as attacks on journalists, genocide, and innocent civilians being unjustly detained. https://t.co/ovtv2uwjD2

The Pacific Security Maritime Exchange was established in 2018 to combat the DPRK’s flagrant sanctions violations. In our latest DipNote blog, @StateISN explains how partner nations are working together to track and prevent the DPRK's illicit activities: https://t.co/atFLdt7SEq

Chag sameach! We wish everyone a happy and healthy Passover. In Jewish tradition, matzah, a flat unleavened bread eaten during the Passover holiday, is known as both the bread of faith and the bread of healing. May faith and actions bring comfort and healing. https://t.co/FSXtcFvULL

Today marks history as we released our first-ever Equity Action Plan—a roadmap to advance racial equity and support for underserved communities worldwide. The U.S. is committed to rooting its foreign policy in equity and inclusion. Read more here: https://t.co/L6xBFQE59j. https://t.co/0mFPqYEran

.@StateDeptSpox discusses the first-ever Equity Action Plan released. “This plan is a product of @POTUS’s historic Executive Order directing federal agencies to address barriers to opportunity for underrepresented and underserved communities.” https://t.co/n6kNujx5GF

.@StateDeptSpox provides an update on State Department Travel Advisories and encourages U.S. citizens to stay connected via https://t.co/G3AYMsK503 and @TravelGov and to enroll in the Smart Traveler Enrollment Program to receive Alerts about evolving health and safety conditions. https://t.co/S8SSUV144V
